LCC-HS9-L4-1400P-HSALG1-DBT-API-02 • HS Math: Algebra I Grade 9 • Solving Linear Equations, Inequalities, and Absolute Value Equations • Debate Duel • Asian & Pacific Islander Narratives

The Beads Slide Both Ways

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What are the students doing?
2QUESTION 2: Why does the girl think distance is a stronger argument?
3QUESTION 3: What does the boy decide to do?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a full justification across all three answers. Name what the students are preparing for, the family source of the girl's reasoning about distance, and the plan the boy commits to. For each, cite the specific evidence in the dialogue and explain the reasoning that links the evidence to the answer.
